health.title: System looks ready
health.summary: No blocking system issue is currently detected.
health.why: The terminal is online, the sync queue is not reporting a critical blocker, and there is no active diagnostic rule that needs operator action.
steps: Refresh status if the indicators on screen look stale.
Export diagnostics before escalating if staff still report a mismatch.
whenToEscalate: Escalate if orders, payments, or shift close behave differently from the status shown here.

printer.title: Printer setup looks stable
printer.summary: No blocking printer issue is currently detected for this view.
printer.why: The selected printer does not match any high-priority troubleshooting rule right now.
steps: Refresh diagnostics if print output does not match this status.
Open printer settings if you need to re-check the assigned transport or default profile.
whenToEscalate: Escalate if receipts still fail, stall, or print with missing formatting.

offline.title: Terminal is offline
offline.summary: This terminal is not connected, so local work will stay queued until connectivity returns.
offline.why: The sync monitor reports no online connection. Orders can remain local, but uploads, reconciliations, and some branch state will lag.
steps: Check the terminal network connection and any local router or hotspot.
Keep taking orders locally, but do not assume central dashboards are current.
Refresh status after connectivity is restored.
whenToEscalate: Escalate if the terminal stays offline after the network is confirmed working.
Escalate immediately if payments or shift close are blocked.

sync_error_active.title: Sync has an active error
sync_error_active.summary: The sync layer is reporting an active failure that needs operator review.
sync_error_active.why: The queue or payment sync reported an error, or failed payment items are waiting. Until that clears, financial and order data can drift from the server.
steps: Refresh status to confirm the failure is still current.
Review the latest queue or payment failure details in diagnostics.
Export diagnostics before broad recovery steps if the issue continues.
whenToEscalate: Escalate if failed payment items remain after refresh.
Escalate before manual cleanup if the error involves payment integrity.

sync_stale.title: Sync telemetry is stale
sync_stale.summary: The terminal is online, but the telemetry snapshot has not updated recently.
sync_stale.why: The last sync timestamps look older than expected, which can mean the sync worker is delayed or the status snapshot is stale.
steps: Refresh status to request a fresh snapshot.
Confirm that the queue is still moving and new orders are syncing.
Export diagnostics if the timestamps remain stale after refresh.
whenToEscalate: Escalate if the timestamps do not advance after repeated refreshes.
Escalate if staff see cross-terminal mismatches at the same time.

backlog_blocked.title: A queued item is blocking progress
backlog_blocked.summary: The sync backlog contains an item that is stuck behind retries or a hard failure.
backlog_blocked.why: The queue monitor reports a blocked entity with retry history. Newer work can pile up behind the failing item until it is resolved.
steps: Refresh status to confirm the blocked item and retry state.
Use the entity id in diagnostics to identify the failing order or record.
Export diagnostics before attempting any manual queue repair.
whenToEscalate: Escalate if the same entity stays blocked across refreshes.
Escalate immediately if the blocked item involves payments or end-of-day data.

financial_queue_failed.title: Financial sync items failed
financial_queue_failed.summary: One or more financial records failed to sync.
financial_queue_failed.why: Driver earnings, staff payments, or shift expenses include failed sync items. Financial data should be treated as incomplete until those failures are reviewed.
steps: Open the financial panel to see which financial queue is affected.
Confirm whether the failed items are growing or already resolved.
Export diagnostics if the failures do not clear after refresh.
whenToEscalate: Escalate before editing totals manually.
Escalate if payroll, expenses, or reconciliation depend on the missing data.

invalid_orders_present.title: Invalid pending orders were detected
invalid_orders_present.summary: The diagnostics layer found pending orders that do not pass validation.
invalid_orders_present.why: One or more locally queued orders contain invalid data. Those orders can block sync or create mismatches until corrected.
steps: Use the affected order ids to identify the problematic tickets.
Refresh status after any correction to confirm that the invalid set shrinks.
Export diagnostics if the same orders reappear or cannot be repaired.
whenToEscalate: Escalate if invalid orders block normal checkout or kitchen flow.
Escalate if the invalid orders appear again after a fresh app restart.

pending_zreport_submit.title: A local Z report still needs submission
pending_zreport_submit.summary: The previous business day is already closed on this terminal, but its local Z submission is still pending.
pending_zreport_submit.why: The frozen end-of-day context has not finished its local Z-report submission yet. This is a closeout state for the previous business day, not a generic sync-health failure.
steps: Refresh status to confirm whether the pending report cleared.
Avoid starting manual reconciliation from another terminal until this is resolved.
Export diagnostics if the pending report date remains stuck.
whenToEscalate: Escalate if the pending report survives reconnects or an app restart.
Escalate before opening a new business-day flow if closeout data looks inconsistent.

not_configured.title: No printer is configured
not_configured.summary: This terminal has no printer profile ready for receipts or tickets.
not_configured.why: The printer settings list is empty, so the POS has nothing local to route print jobs to.
steps: Open quick setup and add the printer used at this terminal.
Confirm the correct role and transport while creating the profile.
Run diagnostics after setup before relying on live printing.
whenToEscalate: Escalate if the printer should already be provisioned for this terminal.
Escalate if setup cannot detect or save the device.

no_default_profile.title: No default printer is assigned
no_default_profile.summary: Printers exist, but none is marked as the default target.
no_default_profile.why: Without a default profile, receipt jobs can route inconsistently or require manual selection.
steps: Edit the printer that should handle primary receipts.
Assign it as the default profile for this terminal.
Refresh diagnostics after saving to confirm the default is active.
whenToEscalate: Escalate if the default flag will not persist.
Escalate if tickets route to the wrong device after assigning a default.

offline_or_error.title: The printer is offline or reporting an error
offline_or_error.summary: The selected printer is not currently healthy enough for reliable output.
offline_or_error.why: Live printer state reports offline or error status, and the device may reject or stall print jobs until connectivity or hardware issues are resolved.
steps: Check power, cable, network reachability, and the printer error indicators.
Refresh printer diagnostics after the device is reachable again.
Review printer settings if the wrong transport or address is assigned.
whenToEscalate: Escalate if the device stays offline after power and network checks.
Escalate immediately if receipts are required and no fallback printer is available.

transport_unresolved.title: Printer transport could not be resolved
transport_unresolved.summary: The POS cannot confirm the actual transport or address for the selected printer.
transport_unresolved.why: Diagnostics could not resolve a reachable transport or address, so the print pipeline cannot prove where jobs should be delivered.
steps: Edit the printer settings and verify the IP, USB, or other transport details.
Run diagnostics again after saving the expected transport.
Use quick setup if the current profile is outdated or incomplete.
whenToEscalate: Escalate if the transport keeps resolving to empty or unreachable values.
Escalate if the printer works manually but the POS cannot resolve it.

unverified.title: Printer setup is not verified
unverified.summary: The printer profile exists, but it has not passed verification yet.
unverified.why: Unverified or unresolved printer state means the saved profile has not completed a clean diagnostic check with the device.
steps: Open quick setup or printer edit to confirm the stored transport values.
Run diagnostics after any change to verify the device.
Use the printer with caution until verification succeeds.
whenToEscalate: Escalate if verification keeps failing on a known-good device.
Escalate if production printing depends on this printer and there is no fallback.

recent_job_failures.title: Recent print jobs failed
recent_job_failures.summary: The printer accepted some work recently, but recent jobs are failing.
recent_job_failures.why: Diagnostics report failed recent jobs or a queue that is not clearing. That usually points to an intermittent device, transport, or profile problem.
steps: Refresh diagnostics to confirm whether failures are still increasing.
Inspect the printer queue and device status for jammed or stalled jobs.
Edit the printer profile if the transport or address may be outdated.
whenToEscalate: Escalate if failures continue after diagnostics refresh.
Escalate if kitchen or receipt output is delayed during service.

degraded.title: Printer is degraded
degraded.summary: The printer is reachable, but the current health state is not fully clean.
degraded.why: The printer is not fully offline, yet diagnostics still report degraded state or reduced confidence in the setup.
steps: Refresh diagnostics to confirm whether the degraded state clears.
Review the printer settings and verification state for incomplete setup.
Keep a fallback printer ready until the status returns to normal.
whenToEscalate: Escalate if degraded state persists across multiple checks.
Escalate if print quality or delivery timing is affected.
